pairs.question: What are the two main SEO-related options available when configuring an RSS feed import on DEV?
pairs.answer: When setting up an RSS feed import, you can choose between two SEO-related options: (1) **Mark the RSS source as canonical URL by default**, which preserves your original content's SEO ranking by making the DEV version canonical, and (2) **Replace self-referential links with DEV Community-specific links**, which updates URLs to point to the imported DEV version of an article instead of the original source.

pairs.question: How does DEV handle imported articles when the original author doesn't have a DEV account?
pairs.answer: If the original author of an imported post does not have a DEV account, the article will be listed under the default author specified during the RSS feed import setup. This ensures that all syndicated content is properly attributed within the DEV community.

pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'published' tag in the markdown of a draft post on DEV?
pairs.answer: The 'published' tag in the markdown of a draft post determines whether the post remains a draft or is published. To publish a post, change the 'published' tag from **false** to **true** and save the changes. This allows users to finalize and publicly share their imported content.

pairs.question: How can you monitor the status of an RSS feed import job on DEV?
pairs.answer: After adding an RSS feed source, a background job is queued to import your posts. You can check the status by refreshing the RSS Import Feeds page after a few minutes. The page will display the import status, including a list of imported drafts, skipped articles, or any errors encountered during the process.

pairs.question: Provide a code snippet demonstrating how to set the 'published' tag to true in a DEV post's markdown.
pairs.answer:
Here is an example of how to modify the 'published' tag in the markdown of a DEV draft post to publish it:

markdown
---
title: My Imported Post
description: A post imported via RSS
published: true
tags: ['imported', 'rss']
---

# My Imported Post

This is the content of my post.


After saving the changes, the post will be published on DEV.

pairs.question: What happens when you enable the 'Replace self-referential links' option during an RSS feed import?
pairs.answer: Enabling the **Replace self-referential links** option updates all URLs in the imported content to point to the corresponding articles on the DEV Community instead of the original external source. This is particularly useful when migrating an entire blog to DEV, as it ensures readers are directed to the DEV version of the content.

pairs.question: How can you assign an imported RSS feed to a specific organization or default author on DEV?
pairs.answer: During the RSS feed import setup, you can assign the imported articles to a specific organization or set a default author. This is done on the RSS Import Feeds page under your Dashboard, where you can select the desired organization and author before saving the feed source. Articles will then be associated with the chosen organization and author during the import process.
